fields.dosage_form: tablet, film coated; tablet for oral suspension; for oral suspension (sprinkle)
fields.boxed_warning_title: (A) PREMATURE DISCONTINUATION OF ELIQUIS INCREASES THE RISK OF THROMBOTIC EVENTS (B) SPINAL/EPIDURAL HEMATOMA
fields.indication_summary: Factor Xa inhibitor indicated to reduce the risk of stroke and systemic embolism in adult patients with nonvalvular atrial fibrillation; for prophylaxis of DVT (which may lead to PE) after hip or knee replacement surgery; for treatment of DVT and PE and reduction in risk of their recurrence following initial therapy; and for treatment of VTE and reduction of recurrent VTE risk in pediatric patients from birth and older.
fields.dosage_forms_strengths: Tablets: 2.5 mg and 5 mg; Tablet For Oral Suspension: 0.5 mg; For Oral Suspension: 0.15 mg in a yellow opaque capsule

notes: First document in this sample set with a two-part lettered boxed warning -- the title is split into '(A)' and '(B)' subheadings rather than a single phrase, which any boxed-warning-title extractor needs to handle. The SPL title also covers a second product listing, 'ELIQUIS 30-DAY STARTER PACK'. Labeler is E.R. Squibb & Sons (BMS side of the BMS-Pfizer alliance; adverse-reaction contact is Bristol-Myers Squibb), not a Pfizer entity. All three revision dates agree on April 2025: PI 'Revised: 4/2025', Medication Guide 'Revised 04/2025' (kept in its printed 04/2025 form), SPL footer 4/2025 -- no cross-date ambiguity. Multiple package NDCs (0003-0893-21 for 2.5 mg 60ct, 0003-0894-21 for 5 mg 60ct, 0003-3764-74 starter pack); no single ndc can represent that, so left null.
